article_title: US Treasury Proposes Stablecoin AML Rules Under GENIUS Act as Bessent Pushes Congress on CLARITY Act

content_preview.title: US Treasury Proposes Stablecoin AML Rules Under GENIUS Act as Bessent Pushes Congress on CLARITY Act
content_preview.summary: FinCEN and OFAC jointly proposed anti-money laundering and sanctions compliance rules for stablecoin issuers on April 8, while Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent urged Congress to advance the CLARITY Act to complete the federal digital asset regulatory framework.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The US Treasury Department moved to implement a key provision of the nation's first stablecoin law on April 8, as the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) and the Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) [jointly proposed anti-money laundering and sanctions compliance rules](https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0435) for permitted payment stablecoin issuers. The rulemaking follows the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for US Stablecoins (GENIUS) Act, wh...
